---
title: "Jacob R. Smith"
knit: (function(inputFile, encoding) {
      out_dir <- "docs";
      rmarkdown::render(inputFile,
                        encoding=encoding,
                        output_dir=file.path(dirname(inputFile), out_dir))})
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

:::: {.hero}
<img src="images/profile.jpg" alt="Jacob R. Smith" class="profile-pic">

::: {.hero-text}
I'm Jacob Smith, an Economics Ph.D. candidate at Middle Tennessee State University (expected 2026) and a Graduate Research Fellow with MTSU's Political Economy Research Institute (PERI). My work sits in applied microeconomics and public choice, with a focus on historical political economy, the political economy of regulation, and economic freedom.

This site collects my research, working papers, teaching, and CV.

<div class="social-row">
  <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/jacobsmithr" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><i class="fab fa-linkedin-in"></i> LinkedIn</a>
  <a href="mailto:jacob.rand.smith@gmail.com"><i class="fas fa-envelope"></i> Email</a>
  <!-- Add your profile URLs and uncomment to display these icons:
  <a href="GOOGLE_SCHOLAR_URL" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><i class="ai ai-google-scholar"></i> Google Scholar</a>
  <a href="ORCID_URL" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><i class="ai ai-orcid"></i> ORCID</a>
  -->
</div>
:::
::::

## Research Interests

Historical political economy, public choice theory, Austrian economics, and the political economy of public-interest regulation.

## Working Papers

- *"The Father of Food and Drug Law as Strategic Regulator: Dr. Harvey Wiley and the Political Economy of the Bottled-in-Bond Act"*
- *"Bank Runs without a Cause: Frequency, Contagion, and Mitigation Measures in the U.S., 1889–1929"*
- *"Alms and Ale: A Malthusian Test of Welfare Generosity and Alcohol Consumption in OECD Countries, 1995–2023"*
- *"Assuring Consumers of Authenticity, Safety, and Quality in Early American Whiskey Markets"*

## About Me

I am a graduate teaching assistant and Ph.D. candidate in economics at Middle Tennessee State University's Jones College of Business, completing my degree in 2026. I am also a Summer 2024 and 2025 Fellow with MTSU's Political Economy Research Institute (PERI), where I collaborate with leading scholars on pressing economic and policy questions.

I began my academic career at Hampden-Sydney College, graduating *magna cum laude* in 2023 with a B.S. in Mathematical Economics and a second major in Philosophy. That interdisciplinary foundation shapes the toolkit and perspective I bring to my teaching and research.

Learn more on my [C.V.](cv.html), [Teaching](teaching.html), and [Research](research.html) pages.

## Contact

Whether you're a fellow researcher, a student, or simply interested in economic questions, I'd be glad to connect. The quickest ways to reach me are below.

- **LinkedIn:** [linkedin.com/in/jacobsmithr](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jacobsmithr)
- **Email:** [jacob.rand.smith@gmail.com](mailto:jacob.rand.smith@gmail.com)
